channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Unlike past eras, anti-Muslim GOP rhetoric draws little pushback from party leaders
item.description: A growing chorus of Republicans in Congress have embraced rhetoric against Muslims and sharia law. But unlike in past years, their remarks have faced little public pushback from leadership.
item.content:encoded: A growing chorus of Republicans in Congress have embraced rhetoric against Muslims and sharia law. But unlike in past years, their remarks have faced little public pushback from leadership.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: States pass laws against health insurers' prior authorization
item.description: More than 30 states have passed laws trying to rein in health insurance companies second guessing doctors, a policy called prior authorization.
item.content:encoded: More than 30 states have passed laws trying to rein in health insurance companies second guessing doctors, a policy called prior authorization.
item.dc:creator: April Dembosky

item.title: New Music Friday: Johnny Blue Skies and the Dark Clouds
item.description: The country singer-songwriter formerly known as Sturgill Simpson has a new album out under his current stage name, Johnny Blue Skies and the Dark Clouds.
item.content:encoded: The country singer-songwriter formerly known as Sturgill Simpson has a new album out under his current stage name, Johnny Blue Skies and the Dark Clouds.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: The Oscars are Sunday. NPR's film critic has thoughts
item.description: From Timothee Chalamet's comments about opera to the new category of Best Casting, here's what to know ahead of the Academy Awards.
item.content:encoded: From Timothee Chalamet's comments about opera to the new category of Best Casting, here's what to know ahead of the Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: Bob Mondello

item.title: Synth-pop duo Haute & Freddy 'dance the pain away' on debut album
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Michelle Buzz and Lance Shipp of the synthpop group Haute & Freddy about the release of their debut album, Big Disgrace.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Michelle Buzz and Lance Shipp of the synthpop group Haute & Freddy about the release of their debut album, Big Disgrace.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Intagliata

item.title: In layers of melting glacier ice, scientists find a story of pre-industrial pollution
item.description: A glacier in Europe holds clues about the activities of pre-industrial people, but it's melting fast.
item.content:encoded: A glacier in Europe holds clues about the activities of pre-industrial people, but it's melting fast.
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: In Portland hearing, DHS testimony shows officer confusion on use of force
item.description: In a recent lawsuit against the federal government's use of tear gas and other crowd control measures on protesters, depositions of DHS officers gave a rare glimpse into federal agents' training.
item.content:encoded: In a recent lawsuit against the federal government's use of tear gas and other crowd control measures on protesters, depositions of DHS officers gave a rare glimpse into federal agents' training.
item.dc:creator: Conrad Wilson

item.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy concerned as U.S. suspends Russia sanctions
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy meets French President Emmanuel Macron in Paris to increase pressure on Russia, as the U.S. suspends oil sanctions.
item.content:encoded: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy meets French President Emmanuel Macron in Paris to increase pressure on Russia, as the U.S. suspends oil sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Life under bombing in Tehran: The diary of an Iranian writer paints a vivid picture
item.description: In written messages and voice notes, one resident of Tehran gives a rare and intimate portrayal of life in the Iranian capital under constant bombardment.
item.content:encoded: In written messages and voice notes, one resident of Tehran gives a rare and intimate portrayal of life in the Iranian capital under constant bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Tick-borne meat allergy can affect livestock producers' health and livelihood
item.description: Alpha-Gal syndrome comes from tick bites. It causes severe allergies to some meat and dairy products. For some farmers and ranchers, it not only affects their health but also their livelihood.
item.content:encoded: Alpha-Gal syndrome comes from tick bites. It causes severe allergies to some meat and dairy products. For some farmers and ranchers, it not only affects their health but also their livelihood.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca A Smith
